column1: # Hand-curated mapping consumed by scripts/license-table.sh. Adding a new
# crate to the workspace REQUIRES adding a row here in the same PR; the
# CI gate (`license-table.sh --check`) refuses to merge otherwise.
#                                  MCP servers — anything that boots and
#   - frontier  → Apache-2.0      (pure libs / SDKs / type crates /
#                                  trait families a third party might
#                                  `cargo add` to build on top of cosmon).
#   « Un tiers ferait-il `cargo add cosmon-X` sans vouloir tout
#     AGPL-iser son projet ? » Yes → frontier ; No → core.
# If unsure, choose core (safe default — preserves the
# flèche-non-inversée invariant). Park the doubt in
# docs/license/temp-warm-list.md for the next operator audit.

column4: name-reservation crate (publish=true) — ships product metadata, AGPL
API — code-links AGPL core/state/filestore, not pure-network (delib-ca76)
Claude bridge — layer ④ engine, code-links core (delib-ca76)
kernel — the moat; AGPL closes the strip-mine hole (delib-ca76)
llama inference wrapper — AGPL Rust binding (off by default)
ops tools — layer ③ product harness (delib-ca76)
process-identity witness for adapters — same kernel tier as the adapters it guards
provider trait — layer ④ engine; closes the --features llama SPDX lie (delib-ca76)
remote CLI — code-links AGPL core+surface-canon, not pure-network (delib-ca76)
state store trait — crash-recovery wedge, layer ② (delib-ca76)
HTTP-on-Tailscale transport — vendored sibling-galaxy lib, Apache ⑦
citation identifier graph data structure (ADR-091 §D3) — pure lib, NDJSON wire format
abstract citation graph trait family (ADR-091 §D6) — explicit open-extensibility seam
read-only Bloom + columnstore lookup lib (ADR-091 §D7) — no network, no binary
raw FFI over vendored llama.cpp (MIT) — Apache wrapper, vendored ⑦
